leadText: Toedscool (Japanese: ノノクラゲ Nonokurage) is a dual-type Ground/Grass Pokémon introduced in Generation IX. It evolves into Toedscruel starting at level 30. It bears a close resemblance to Tentacool; however, the two are separate species.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Toedscool is a Pokémon that is similar to a mushroom. It has a bulbous pink body with a yellow dot just above its eyes, and two larger yellow circles located further above on either side of its head. Toedscool has small pupils on its eyes and its lower body flares out into frayed flaps. These flaps sometimes fall off from its body, and have a chewy and delicious taste; as such, they are used in recipes such as pickles. Toedscool has two long, thin, white legs resembling tentacles or mycelium , which it uses to run at a speed of 30 mph (50 km/h). Toedscool lives in humid forests, where it clings to tree roots. Although Toedscool physically resembles Tentacool , the two species are biologically unrelated. Toedscool and its Evolution, Toedscruel , are the only known Pokémon that can have Mycelium Might as an Ability . Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Toedscool evolves into Toedscruel . (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Toedscool Ground Grass → First Evolution Toedscruel Ground Grass

Generation IX Paldea #244 Kitakami #74 Blueberry #— Lumiose #— Hyperspace #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Legends: Z-A . Scarlet Toedscool lives in muggy forests. The flaps that fall from its body are chewy and very delicious. Violet Though it looks like Tentacool , Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph . Spin-off games Pokopia #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Pokopia . sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode Pokémon Source Entry HZ051 Toedscool Liko's Rotom Phone Toedscool, the Woodear Pokémon. A Ground and Grass type. They make their homes in damp forest land. Although their legs seem thin, they can run up to 30 miles per hour .
